meta.name: 10 Mile Beginner
meta.subtitle: Your first 10-mile race in 14 weeks
meta.description: For runners who have completed a 10k and want to step up to 10 miles. Fourteen weeks of progressive base building, with the long run building to 19km to ensure race confidence.
meta.coach_notes: The step from 10k to 10 miles is mostly about the long run. The Thursday steady sessions build endurance economy; the long Sunday runs build the raw ability to cover the distance. Run the easy days at a pace where you could hold a full conversation without any effort.

weeks.title: Base week 1
weeks.note: Week 1. The long run on Sunday is the most important session — keep it easy enough to hold a full conversation. The Thursday run builds steady-state endurance.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

2km WU · 4km at steady effort (5:30–5:50/km) · 1km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.
8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

11km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational throughout — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Base week 2
weeks.note: Week 2. The long run on Sunday is the most important session — keep it easy enough to hold a full conversation. The Thursday run builds steady-state endurance.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

2km WU · 5km at steady effort (5:30–5:50/km) · 1km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.
9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

12km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational throughout — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

13km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational throughout — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Deliberate recovery week. Volume drops by 35%. This is when the adaptation from the first three weeks consolidates. Rest well.

sessions.det: 6km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
6km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
5km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
6km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
12km easy · Comfortable and relaxed — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

weeks.title: Development week 1
weeks.note: Week 5. Long run reaches 14km this Sunday — keep the pace easy and the effort comfortable.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 5x800m at 10k pace (90sec rec) · 2km CD · 10km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Pull-ups 4x8 · DB row 4x8 · Overhead press 4x8 · Face pulls 4x8 · Core circuit 4x8s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

2km WU · 20min tempo (5:10–5:25/km) · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.
9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

14km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Build endurance at easy effort — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Development week 2
weeks.note: Week 6. Long run reaches 15km this Sunday — keep the pace easy and the effort comfortable.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 5x800m at 10k pace (90sec rec) · 2km CD · 10km total — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
2km WU · 20min tempo (5:10–5:25/km) · 2km CD — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.
9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

15km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Build endurance at easy effort — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Race prep week 1
weeks.note: Race preparation week 1. The long run reduces as we approach race day — this is deliberate. Your legs need to arrive fresh.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

2km WU · 18min at steady race pace effort · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.
8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 18min at steady race pace effort · 2km CD — A short threshold session to maintain the feel of race effort. Comfortably hard — not racing.
8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
17km easy · Comfortable and controlled — A confidence long run in taper — resist the urge to push. Arrive at the start line rested, not tired from a final fitness test.

weeks.title: Race week
weeks.note: Race week. Rest, eat well, sleep well. The fitness is built — trust it.

sessions.det: 5km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
4km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
3km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

1.5km WU · 4x100m strides · Race: first 3 miles at comfortable effort, build from mile 7 · Trust your long run training — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Porridge with banana or toast with peanut butter. 500ml water. Only familiar foods.
200ml water. Warm up well — 15min easy, strides. For 10-mile and half, a proper warm-up is essential.
Water at every station. For 10-mile: gel at 6–7 miles if trained with gels. For half: gel at miles 6 and 10.
Water immediately. 500ml in 10 minutes. Banana or protein snack. Full meal within 90 minutes.
Proper meal with protein, carbohydrates, and whatever you have been looking forward to. Well earned.
